claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 4 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 4 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Now I have a few more questions to help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Does Alex live with you, or do you pay at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards his upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money)?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a couple more questions:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! One final question to complete the assessment:

**Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a UK citizen, do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another immigration status that gives you the right to reside?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a UK citizen, so I have the right to reside in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 4 years old (under 16, meeting the age requirement)
- You live in the UK and are a UK citizen (meeting residency requirements)
- Alex lives with you (meaning you're responsible for him)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim if you haven't already done so.

**Please note:** If you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 4 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside as a UK citizen. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for Alex under the Child Benefit rules. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 4-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as he lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility requirements under the Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 4 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside as a UK citizen. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for Alex under the Child Benefit rules. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 4-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as he lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility requirements under the Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
